transcript.clean_text: foreign is live and On Demand each and every day of the race watch every kilometer on gcn plus stage six of the race took the Peloton to Naples a city celebrating after its football team did the almost Unthinkable among the Italian championship for the first time in 33 years Caden Grove should won his first jeded Italia stage Jay Vine had lost a little bit of time Mark Canada Should Crash the day before and was suffering with a little bit of a knee injury and Remco world champion had also been down on a rainy day in Salerno thankfully for stage six the sunshine was out and it was one of the shorter days of the general d'Italia the third shortest Road stage in fact at 162 kilometers including some tough terrain Vali korikionsi the beautiful amalfico store was turning left and right piccot santangelo before through Sorrento for the final intermediate Sprint and then a run along the Bay of Naples just in front of Mount Vesuvius before coming into enables for the Finish a Sprint maybe one for the Breakaway we were left waiting and getting at the start the start was an aggressive one Jacob alula Keen to get out there Alessandro de Marquis one of the more active Riders he'd soon be joined by four other Riders and we'd have a break of five going up the road gavazi quarterman Clark and duletro joining him Clark the Australian headline name alongside the Marquis riding for an Aussie teal the intermediate Sprint behind was taken mainly by Mars Peterson Jonathan Milan up there Caden Groves missing out on points though Milan still leading in the Maya chiklamino Mark Cavendish would be dropped from the main Peloton suffering with that knee injury and the climb as in your screen of years up the pace on the first categorized climb of the day and then it was time for the scenery viewers around the world able to sit back and watch in the Breakaway too many were sitting back and watching as Clark and demarkey proved the stronger Riders on the second categorized climb they go away alone and still have over two minutes on the Peloton Connecticut and a few others were already out a possible Sprint game here the rest of the break was caught 50k's remained question is could the break hang on and they were looking good just over a minute with 15 kilometers to go and the Peloton not able to get fully organized mechanical problems for primar schrodlich and then Garrett Thomas meant that it would be a difficult time for those hoping to defend General classification both will be eventually held back on Garrett Thomas needing a team time trial through the cars to get there get there he would though but would the Peloton get to the Breakaway still 33 seconds with three kilometers to go and it was the breaks race to throw away Clark looking for a first ever jet at Italia stage and wanting to complete the grand tour set the Marquis a former Maya Rosa looking to add a zero stage to his three taken in the Welter they LED with 800 meters to go but they waited they watched they played games and one game too many meant that it would be heartbreak for the Breakaway with 200 meters to go Fernando gabillia launching the Sprint coming around in would be Mass Peterson Milan trying to do the same but it would be Peterson holding it to the line Mars Peterson finally winning the stage completing the Peter slam a grand slam of stages in all three grand Tours a first Danish stage win at the general Italia for 11 Seasons from Lars back to mass Peterson and with Jonathan Milan coming in and picking up second place he would keep the mariachi clamino a good third place for Pascal Ackerman who's looking strong Caton Groves with fourth Victory going to track Sega Fredo the team that had taken so much control of this race but the depths of disappointment for Simon Clark the 36 year old from Melbourne Mass Peterson beating Milan Ackerman Groves and gavilia to the win more celebrations for Andreas leknerson he keeps the Scandinavian theme going by equaling knutson's three days in the mayarosa a big day coming up for him on stage seven leading by 28 seconds from Remco ivanapol and 30 from ohelia in the top 10 still separated by 139 so to Stage seven and the first real mountain GC test a monster of a stage going through Castel Di sangro and taking on two more classified climbs a beast through the apennines 219 kilometers long and finishing high up at Grand Sasso d'Italia Campo imperator as always you'll be able to watch it live as the drama unfolds and if not on catch up with gcn plus the Gerald Italia but something happens every day
